title: buildah rm nonexistent-container: mount leak
body:
This is why buildah BATS tests fail using overlay. (Or at least it's one reason).
```console
# mkdir -p /var/tmp/mytmp/{,run}root
# buildah --root /var/tmp/mytmp/root --runroot /var/tmp/mytmp/runroot from alpine
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 89d9c30c1d48 done
Copying config 965ea09ff2 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
alpine-working-container
# buildah --root /var/tmp/mytmp/root --runroot /var/tmp/mytmp/runroot commit --rm alpine-working-container alpine-image
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 77cae8ab23bf skipped: already exists
Copying blob 6b316488d473 done
Copying config 4f38e26433 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
4f38e26433f6d044f6228b622f4186e49b0bf2f43c97a93125016012821854d1

# buildah --root /var/tmp/mytmp/root --runroot /var/tmp/mytmp/runroot rm alpine-working-container
error removing container "alpine-working-container": error reading build container: container not known
^^^^ this is expected: the commit was done with --rm

# rm -rf /var/tmp/mytmp
rm: cannot remove '/var/tmp/mytmp/root/overlay': Device or resource busy
^^^ this is not expected.
# mount|grep mytmp
/dev/vda1 on /var/tmp/mytmp/root/overlay type ext4 (rw,relatime,seclabel)
```

comments.body: I am not sure this is  a new leak.  But we did remove some functionality from Overlay to Skip local mount.  Which might be what is causing this.

What I didn't mention is that the `rm -rf` works fine immediately after the `commit`. That is: somewhere in the `buildah rm` a mount is happening, and is not getting cleaned up in the error exit. I think.
@edsantiago do you know if this is still an issue?
I was just able to reproduce it on my laptop, buildah-1.15.0-1.fc32, using the steps in the original comment
Storage shutdown should fix this one also.
